steps: Register a web app (confidential client) in Microsoft Entra ID.
Grant it Partner Center API permissions and obtain admin consent.
Request a token from {authority}/{tenantId}/oauth2/token using grant_type=client_credentials with resource https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com.
Prefer a certificate over a client secret for the credential.
app-only.supportedNote: Not all operations support app-only; check each scenario's authType. Customer user, license, service request, and analytics operations require app+user. Native/public-client apps support app+user only.

steps: Use the Secure Application Model (refresh-token based). Register a multi-tenant web app.
Perform an interactive partner consent with MFA to get an authorization code, exchange it for a refresh token, and store the refresh token in a secure vault (e.g. Azure Key Vault).
Exchange the refresh token for an access token with resource https://api.partnercenter.microsoft.com (grant_type=refresh_token).
Call Partner Center with the access token. For OBO/customer-tenant admin operations, a GDAP relationship + security group membership grants the least-privileged roles.
app+user.secureAppModel: Required for app+user. GDAP (granular delegated admin privileges) replaced open-ended DAP: request specific, time-bound Entra roles per customer. The calling user must be in the AdminAgents group and in a security group tied to the customer's GDAP relationship with the needed role (e.g. User Administrator, Directory Writers). See enable-secure-app-model and gdap-and-secure-application-model.

mfa.note: From 2026-04-01 all App+User usage of Partner Center APIs enforces MFA. From October 2025 the API confirms presence of the MFA claim. Send ValidateMfa: true to have the response report isMfaCompliant, and use GET /v1/partnerRequests to find non-compliant callers (mfaCompliant per request).

deprecations.fix: Use the Partner Center REST APIs directly.
Migrate to granular, time-bound GDAP relationships and request only the Entra roles each operation needs.
Use MSAL with the secure application model.
Use Microsoft.Graph PowerShell (and the community Partner Center PowerShell module) instead.
